Off Calendar Jan-31-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS CONSERVATION AND LIQUIDATION OFFICE AND SCOTT PEARCE'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT.
APPEARANCES: GOLNAR J. FOZI, ESQUIRE (DALEY & HEFT, 858.755.5666) APPEARING THROUGH COURTCALL FOR DEFENDANTS CONSERVATION & LIQUIDATION OFFICE AND SCOTT PEARCE. THERE ARE NO OTHER APPEARANCES.
RULING: COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: OVERRULED, TEN DAYS TO ANSWER. THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION ALLEGES A F.E.H.A. CLAIM AND THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION ALLEGES A TAMENY CLAIM. THE DECISION IN WALRATH V SPRINKEL (2002) 99 CAL APP 4TH 1237 HOLDS THAT A SUPERVISOR MAY BE LIABLE FOR RETALIATION. AS TO THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION, F.E.H.A. IS A REMEDIAL STATUTE AND DOES NOT DISPLACE COMMON LAW TORTS. THE FACTS ALLEGED PLEAD OUTRAGEOUS CONDUCT. ALTHOUGH THE FOURTH AND FIFTH CAUSES OF ACTION HAVE SIMILAR ALLEGATIONS, THE COURT CANNOT DETERMINE FROM THE PLEADINGS WHETHER THE CLAIMS ARE DUPLICATIVE. (302/PJM/PB) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600
